nameMeanings.name: Gulby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably a shortened form of English Gooldsbury, a variant of Goldsborough .

variants.name: Goldsborough
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places in North Yorkshire called Goldsborough. One, near Knaresborough, is named from the Old English personal name Godel + Old English burh ‘fortified place’. The other, near Whitby, is named from the Old English personal name Golda + burh.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Gulby was born about 1325, in Speldhurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son.
